description_long: This object is a vintage biplane with a boxy fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires. The wings are fabric-covered and supported by a lightweight frame, with the upper and lower wings separated by vertical struts. The cockpit is open and centrally located, with a simple seat and basic controls visible. The tail section features a rudimentary horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin, with exposed framework and control surfaces. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable, giving the airplane a classic early-aviation appearance.
description: A vintage biplane with fabric-covered straight wings, open cockpit, exposed framework, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, open cockpit, and exposed framework of the biplane.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual wings, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear beneath the fuselage.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the undercarriage, tail skid, and structural struts between the wings.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the open cockpit from behind.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings in a staggered arrangement, no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, centrally located cockpit in the mid-fuselage, single-seat configuration.
